front: Work: the title characters of this poem go to sea in a 'pea-green boat' and eat 'mince and slices of quince' with a 'runcible spoon'
back: The Owl and the Pussycat (Edward Lear)

front: Work: creatures 'went to sea in a Sieve' despite reservations from their friends in this poem
Work: the title character gathers bark of the Tangum tree to construct an apparatus with a lamp inside, after being spurned by a girl with sky-blue hands and green hair
back: The Dong with a Luminous Nose (Edward Lear)
work: The Dong with a Luminous Nose

front: Author: this Victorian nonsense poet maintained a long correspondence with Emily Tennyson and composed music for Alfred Tennyson's poems
Author: this poet named his villa in Sanremo after Emily Tennyson and composed a lullaby-like setting of 'Sweet and Low'
Work: a poem by this author describes the King and Queen of the pelicans hosting a party on the banks of the Nile for daughter Nell
